title: Tracing Garbage Collection and Conservative Limits · Failure Case: Reachability Roots
summary: A language runtime must reclaim cyclic object graphs while preserving roots held by stacks, globals, and foreign code. Planned artifact: Add and verify the cmem reachability roots increment in small mark-and-sweep collector model. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define objects, edges, roots, reachability, and collection safety independently from application usefulness.
Evaluate atomicity, contention, batching, deferred release, and memory-order requirements for shared counts.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: Predict Reachability Roots · Theory Reachability Roots
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory native-systems team handles memory case 8267. Produce bounded C evidence to mark reachable objects and count only unreachable sweep candidates; change the size, lifetime, failure, or ownership condition; print the observed result and invariant. Instant code stays inside the PicoC practice subset. C23 syntax, current GCC and Clang, optimization, ABI, threads, sanitizers, analyzers, fuzzers, operating-system resources, foreign runtimes, load, and production behavior require named controlled transfer gates.
